description_long: This is a classic biplane with a straight, dual-wing configuration and a narrow fuselage, reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft. The upper and lower wings are connected by a series of struts and wires, providing structural integrity and a vintage aesthetic. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers toward the tail, which features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, likely accommodating a single pilot. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels and a small tailwheel, suitable for grass or dirt runways. The exterior is primarily white with a prominent orange stripe running along the top of the wings and fuselage.
description: A vintage biplane with dual straight wings, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear, featuring a white and orange color scheme.
description_view_0: Side view showing the narrow fuselage, open cockpit, and the orange stripe running along the upper wing and fuselage.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the dual straight wings, struts, and the symmetrical biplane structure.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the tail and landing gear.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the fixed landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by struts; no winglets or noticeable dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a small tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward on the fuselage.
